chunks.text: IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Cr. Rev. No. S – 35 of 2022 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge Hearing of case 1. For orders on office objection at flag ‘A’ 2. For hearing of main case 31.10.2024 Syed Sardar Ali Shah Rizvi, Addl. PG for the State >>>>>..<<<<< O R D E R ZULFIQAR ALI SANGI, J;- In compliance of order dated 17.10.2024, SIP Aijaz Ali Jiskani of Police Station Bagerji has submitted reply of show-cause notice along with certain FIRs and submits that the applicant, namely Naveed S/o Sajjan Jatoi and his surety Faheem Ahmed S/o Muhammad Qasim have absconded away and there is no chance in near future for their arrest. This Criminal Revision Application was filed by the applicant against the conviction and sentence awarded to him by the Court of IInd. Assistant Sessions Judge, Sukkur vide judgment dated 19.05.2021 and was maintained by the Appellate Court. The applicant after the grant of bail by this Court has become fugitive from law. In the case of Ikramullah and others vs. The State (2015 SCMR 1002) the Honourable Supreme Court has settled the law that fugitive from law loses his right of audience before a Court. In view of the dicta laid down by the Supreme Court, this Criminal Revision Application is, therefore, dismissed due to above mentioned conduct of the applicant with a clarification that if the applicant is recaptured by the authorities or he surrenders to custody then he may apply for resurrection of his Application. Judge ARBROHI
